industrial_core.guide.name: Voltex Manual
industrial_core.guide.subtitle: Industrial Core
industrial_core.guide.landing: Welcome, engineer.$(br2)This manual is the in-game companion to $(thing)Voltex$() — generating Forge Energy, processing ores, automating logistics, and building toward nuclear power.$(br2)Pick a category below. JEI shows exact recipes; this book explains how the pieces fit together.
industrial_core.guide.cat.getting_starte: Welcome to Voltex, the bootstrap tools, and your first power.
industrial_core.guide.cat.materials.desc: Ores, worldgen, the resin-to-rubber chain, dusts, alloys, components and tools.
industrial_core.guide.cat.energy.desc: Generation, cables, batteries, the charger and charging packs.
industrial_core.guide.cat.machines.desc: Processing, fluids, heat, fabrication and matter generation.
industrial_core.guide.cat.automation.des: Pipe-free logistics, machine upgrades and the capsule system.
industrial_core.guide.cat.equipment.desc: Drills, the wrench, energy armor sets, crates and barrels.
industrial_core.guide.cat.multiblocks.de: The 3x3x3 Miner and the Nuclear Reactor.
industrial_core.guide.entry.welcome.0: $(bold)Voltex$() is an industrial tech mod built on Forge 1.20.1, powered end to end by $(thing)FE$() energy.$(br2)The road ahead is a ladder. You start with simple $(thing)hand tools$(), bend metal into $(thing)Machine Casings$(), and spark your first power. From there you climb: $(li)materials$() $(li)energy$() $(li)machines$() $(li)automation$() $(li)equipment$()$(br2)At the top wait $(o)nuclear$() reactors and $(o)matter generation$(). This book walks the whole climb.
industrial_core.guide.entry.welcome.1: The core loop is familiar: $(thing)ore$() $(bold)->$() $(thing)dust$() $(bold)->$() $(thing)ingot$() $(bold)->$() $(thing)plate$() $(bold)->$() $(thing)components$(). Washing ore in the $(thing)Ore Washer$() yields 3x dust plus a byproduct; rolling plates doubles your metal again.$(br2)$(bold)One gotcha up front:$() only the $(thing)Coal Generator$(), $(thing)Solar Panels$() (T1-T10) and $(thing)Panel Combiner$() actually produce FE.$(br2)The $(o)Lava$() and $(o)Water Generators$() are misnomers — they $(bold)consume$() FE to make fluid and generate $(bold)zero$() power. Likewise the $(thing)Thermogenerator$() burns FE to make heat for the $(thing)Blast Furnace$() and $(thing)Liquefier$().
The $(thing)Hammer$() is your first and most-used tool — it flattens $(thing)ingots$() into $(thing)plates$(), the building block of every machine.$(br2)It is a $(o)reusable$() tool with $(bold)128$() durability. Each plate you craft ticks it down by $(bold)1$(), but it is $(bold)never consumed$() as an ingredient — it stays in the grid, ready for the next craft.
$(bold)Hammer$(): $(bold)4$() iron ingots and $(bold)1$() stick.$(br2)Keep it in your crafting grid — you will reach for it constantly.

$(bold)Wire Cutters$(): $(bold)3$() iron ingots and $(bold)1$() stick.$(br2)Where the Hammer makes plates, the Cutters snip $(thing)plates$() into $(thing)bare wires$() — the start of every cable and electronic part.

Both tools work the same way — drop them in a $(o)shapeless$() recipe alongside the material:$(br)$(li)$(thing)Hammer$() + $(thing)ingot$() $(bold)->$() $(thing)plate$()$(li)$(thing)plate$() + $(thing)Cutters$() $(bold)->$() $(thing)bare wire$()$(br2)Plates and wires exist for iron, copper, gold, bronze, lead and tin.$(br2)Each craft costs $(bold)1$() of $(bold)128$() durability, and the tool survives in the grid. Keep one of each on hand at all times.

$(bold)Machine Casing$(): a ring of $(bold)8$() $(thing)iron plates$() around an empty centre.$(br2)This is the universal chassis. Nearly every machine in Voltex is built on top of it — and it is the next step toward your first $(thing)Coal Generator$() and first power.$(br2)$(o)Tip: it has strength 3.0 and must be mined with the correct tool.$()
industrial_core.guide.entry.ores_worldge: Voltex adds four ore families, each with a $(thing)Deepslate$() variant. All ores drop XP and their $(o)raw$() item; $(bold)Silk Touch$() drops the block, and $(thing)Fortune$() multiplies the raw drop.$(br2)$(li)$(thing)Tin Ore$() — str 3.0, XP 1-3, drops $(thing)Raw Tin$()$(li)$(thing)Lead Ore$() — str 3.0, XP 1-3, drops $(thing)Raw Lead$()$(li)$(thing)Bauxite Ore$() — str 3.0, XP 0-2, drops $(thing)Raw Aluminum$() $(o)(bauxite is the aluminum ore)$()$(li)$(thing)Uranium Ore$() — str 4.0, XP 2-5, drops $(thing)Raw Uranium$()$(br2)Deepslate variants are tougher (str 4.5-5.5) and give slightly more XP.

Each ore generates in its own height band.$(br2)$(li)$(thing)Tin$() & $(thing)Lead$() (vein 9) favor $(o)mountain peaks$(): small count 10 at Y -64..72, middle count 10 at Y -24..54, upper count 90 at Y 80..320, skewed high.$(li)$(thing)Bauxite$() (vein 8): small count 6 at Y -48..60, middle count 7 at Y -16..64, upper count 40 at Y 64..256.$(li)$(thing)Uranium$() (vein 4): a single placement, count 4 at Y -64..10 — $(bold)deep and rare$().$(br2)Veins discard nothing on air exposure, so exposed ore still generates.

$(thing)Raw Ore Blocks$() compress nine raw items into one block for tidy storage, with a shapeless bench recipe in both directions (9 raw $(o)<->$() 1 block). Strength 5.0.$(br2)Available for $(thing)Tin$(), $(thing)Lead$() and $(thing)Uranium$() — there is $(o)no$() raw bauxite block. Purely for compaction; no processing bonus.
$(thing)Rubber$() is the universal insulator — it goes into every cable, electric motor, battery, puller/pusher and barrel. It starts at the $(thing)Resin Tree$(), which generates on the ocean floor at a rarity of $(bold)~1 per 22 chunks$().$(br2)$(thing)Resin Logs$() carry a $(o)latex_state$() (0-2). Tap a log to set it to state 1; on random ticks it has a $(bold)1-in-11$() chance to advance to state 2, when a resin bulb is ready to harvest.

The $(thing)Resin Collector$() automates tapping. Place it $(o)flush against$() a resin log — it auto-faces the log and self-destructs if the log is removed.$(br2)Every $(bold)100 ticks (5 s)$() it scans the trunk upward for the first log at latex_state 2, pulls $(bold)1-3 Tree Resin$(), and resets that log to state 1 to regrow. No power required.

$(thing)Tree Resin$() becomes $(thing)Rubber$() three ways:$(br2)$(li)Smelt $(thing)Tree Resin$() $(o)->$() 1 Rubber (200t)$(li)Smelt a $(thing)Resin Log$() $(o)->$() 1 Rubber$(li)$(thing)Extractor$(): 1 Tree Resin $(o)->$() $(bold)3 Rubber$() (150t, 30 FE)$(br2)Switch to the $(thing)Extractor$() once you have power — it triples yield. Tree Resin also crafts into a $(thing)Slime Ball$() (1:1) for early slime.
industrial_core.guide.entry.dusts_crushi: Dusts are the $(o)ore-doubling$() layer — smelt a dust for double the metal of a raw ingot.$(br2)The $(thing)Crusher$() gives $(bold)1 dust$() per item (200t @ 30 FE) for iron, gold, copper, tin, lead, diamond, lapis, coal, emerald, netherite, aluminum and silicon. Some run special: $(thing)Uranium Dust$() (raw $(o)->$() 2, 60 FE) and $(thing)Amethyst Dust$() (shard $(o)->$() 2, 40 FE).$(br2)The $(thing)Ore Washer$() does better: ore $(o)->$() $(bold)3 dust$() plus a chance byproduct, using 1000 mB water.

Three dusts have $(bold)no direct recipe$() — they only drop as $(thing)Ore Washer$() byproducts:$(br2)$(li)$(thing)Nickel Dust$() — wash iron ore 30% / raw iron 25%$(li)$(thing)Silver Dust$() — wash gold ore 20% / raw gold 15% / raw lead 25%$(li)$(thing)Lithium Dust$() — wash copper ore 25% / raw copper 20%$(br2)These gate the $(thing)Invar$(), $(thing)Electrum$() and superconductor lines. Separately, $(thing)Mineral Dust$() drops from washed sand at $(bold)40%$() (200t @ 40 FE/t) and feeds $(thing)Drill Fluid$().

$(thing)Bronze Dust$() is the only alloy dust made on the bench: $(bold)1 Tin Dust + 3 Copper Dust$() $(o)->$() $(bold)4 Bronze Dust$() (shapeless).$(br2)Smelt the result into $(thing)Bronze Ingots$() — your first proper alloy and a staple for early plates.
industrial_core.guide.entry.alloys_ingot: Beyond smelt-only ingots (Tin, Lead, Aluminum, Bronze, Nickel, Silver), Voltex has a ladder of advanced alloys. $(thing)Kronit$() and $(thing)Kronium Alloy$() are the endgame crafting glue.$(br2)$(thing)Kronit$() has two routes:$(li)$(thing)Restructor$(): 1 iron block + resonant catalyst $(o)->$() 1 (400t, $(bold)25M FE$())$(li)$(thing)Restructor$(): 1 exotic matter + resonant catalyst $(o)->$() $(bold)4$() (400t, 5M FE)$(br2)Once you have a matter farm, the exotic-matter route is far cheaper per Kronit.

$(thing)Blast Furnace$() alloying is unusual: the second metal is consumed as $(bold)FUEL$(), not placed beside the first, and the furnace needs heat from a $(thing)Thermogenerator$().$(br2)$(li)$(thing)Invar$(): iron + nickel $(o)(fuel)$() $(o)->$() 2, 300t, heat 1600$(li)$(thing)Electrum$(): gold + silver $(o)(fuel)$() $(o)->$() 2, 300t, heat 1200$(li)$(thing)Hardened Steel$(): iron + coals $(o)(fuel)$() $(o)->$() 1, 300t, heat 2400

$(thing)Kronium Alloy$() is the glue for T6+ components and armor.$(br2)Bench shaped: $(bold)2 Kronit + 2 Crystal Plate + 1 Plutonium$() $(o)->$() 1.$(br2)$(thing)Plutonium$() itself comes from the $(thing)Restructor$() (4 uranium + resonant catalyst, 400t/10M FE $(o)->$() 1), and $(thing)MOX Fuel$() is a bench craft of 1 plutonium + 2 uranium dust $(o)->$() 2.

$(thing)Composite Ingot$() is a bench craft: $(bold)3 Iron Plate + 3 Bronze Plate + 3 Tin Plate$() $(o)->$() 1.$(br2)$(o)Note:$() this is $(bold)not$() the same as the compressed $(thing)Composite$() material used in logic boards — keep the two straight when following recipes.
industrial_core.guide.entry.plates_compo: Roll ingots in the $(thing)Metal Former$() for $(bold)2 plates$() each (200t, 50 FE) — twice the $(thing)Hammer$()'s 1-per-ingot. This works for iron, copper, gold, bronze, lead, tin, aluminum, electrum, invar and netherite.$(br2)$(o)Gem plates$() are different — they are $(bold)compressed from dusts$(): Diamond Plate (4 dust), Emerald Plate (4 dust), Lapis Plate (8 dust).$(br2)Plates also $(o)extrude$() into bare wires (e.g. iron plate $(o)->$() 4 bare ferro wire) for cables.

The $(thing)carbon chain$() turns coal into structural plating:$(br2)$(li)$(bold)4 Coal Dust$() $(o)->$() 1 $(thing)Carbon Fiber$()$(li)2 Carbon Fiber $(o)->$() 1 $(thing)Carbon Mesh$()$(li)Compress 1 Mesh $(o)->$() $(thing)Carbon Plate$() (100t, 50 FE)$(li)Compress 1 Composite Ingot $(o)->$() $(thing)Composite$() (200t, 50 FE)$(br2)Composite feeds logic boards and high-tier panels.

The $(thing)crystal chain$() yields premium components:$(br2)$(li)Amethyst shard $(o)->$() $(thing)Amethyst Dust$() (crush x2)$(li)$(o)->$() $(thing)Crystal Ingot$() (Restructor or Blast Furnace)$(li)Extrude 1 ingot $(o)->$() 2 $(thing)Crystal Fiber$() (200t, 80 FE)$(li)Compress 4 fiber $(o)->$() 1 $(thing)Crystal Plate$() (400t, 120 FE)$(li)Compress 4 Crystal Ingot $(o)->$() 1 $(thing)Amethyst Crystal$() (400t, 150 FE)$(br2)These feed matrices, lenses and armor.

The electronics ladder climbs from $(thing)Textolite Board$() (4 silicon + 4 bare copper wire + 1 redstone $(o)->$() 2) to the $(thing)Logic Board$() shown here.$(br2)$(thing)Logic Board$(): $(bold)4 Composite + 2 Gold Plate + 4 Textolite Board + 1 Redstone$().$(br2)From it branch the $(thing)Microcontroller$() (4 redstone + 4 silicon + 1 logic board) and the $(thing)Data Transfer Matrix$().

The $(thing)Data Transfer Matrix$() is the $(bold)T6 gateway$() component.$(br2)Bench: $(bold)4 Electrum Plate + 2 Amethyst Crystal + 4 Crystal Fiber + 1 Logic Board$().$(br2)It powers the $(thing)Matter Fabricator$(), Quantum Capacitor, logistics network and high-tier armor — build a steady supply before pushing into T6+.
industrial_core.guide.entry.tools.0: The $(thing)Electric Drill$() is your first powered tool. It holds a $(bold)10,000 FE$() buffer, costs $(bold)200 FE$() per block, and mines at $(thing)Diamond$() tier.$(br2)Speed is $(bold)6.0$() when charged, dropping to $(bold)1.0$() when empty. It has $(o)no durability$() — energy is the only resource — and digs both pickaxe and shovel blocks. Recharge it in a Battery Charger or any FE source.
industrial_core.guide.entry.tools.1: The $(thing)Industrial Drill$() upgrades the formula: tool base + netherite tip + logic board.$(br2)$(bold)50,000 FE$() buffer, $(thing)Netherite$() tier, speed $(bold)18.0$() charged. It has two modes toggled by keybind:$(li)$(bold)1x1$() — 200 FE/block$(li)$(bold)3x3$() — 300 FE/block (pickaxe/shovel blocks only in the area)$(br2)A full 3x3 swing can cost up to $(bold)2,700 FE$(), so switch back to 1x1 to conserve energy. No durability.
industrial_core.guide.entry.tools.2: The $(thing)Electric Wrench$() is the safe way to relocate machines. $(bold)Shift + right-click$() any Voltex machine to pick it up $(o)with its full inventory preserved$() (anvil sound confirms).$(br2)Each use costs $(bold)500 FE$() from a $(bold)20,000 FE$() buffer; below 500 FE it refuses and shows red. Always carry a charged wrench before moving a built-out machine.
industrial_core.guide.entry.tools.3: All three tools share the same logic: they run on $(o)energy, never lose durability$(), show a teal energy bar, and recharge in a $(thing)Battery Charger$() or any FE source.$(br2)They also share components — the $(thing)Tool Base$() (4 iron plate + electric motor + battery) and the drill tips ($(thing)Iron$()/$(thing)Netherite Drill Tip$()) are the building blocks for the drills and wrench alike.
industrial_core.guide.entry.coal_generat: The $(thing)Coal Generator$() is your first true power source. It burns furnace fuel to fill an internal $(thing)50,000 FE$() buffer, then auto-pushes up to $(thing)2,000 FE/t$() into neighbours on all 6 sides.$(br2)It is a generator only: it $(o)cannot$() receive energy. It has a single fuel slot which automation can fill but never empty.
Crafted from $(thing)iron plates$(), a $(thing)furnace$() and a $(thing)machine casing$().

$(bold)Fuel economy:$()$(br)$(li)$(thing)Coal / Charcoal$(): 50 FE/t for 20s = $(thing)20,000 FE$() each.$(li)$(thing)Coal Block$(): 500 FE/t for 20s = $(thing)200,000 FE$() — 10x a coal item in the same window, the efficient choice.$(br2)It accepts $(o)any$() vanilla fuel at a flat 50 FE/t (only burn duration scales), but only the Coal Block gets the 500 FE/t bonus. Burning halts once the 50k buffer is full.
industrial_core.guide.entry.solar_panels: $(thing)Solar Panels$() come in 10 tiers. Each needs $(o)open sky$() (it checks the block above) — blocked or underground means $(bold)0 FE$(). They are generators only and self-push the entire buffer every tick.$(br2)$(bold)Key rule:$() night output is exactly $(thing)one quarter$() of daytime, on every tier — but it still needs open sky at night.

The chain starts with the $(thing)T1 Silicon Solar Panel$(): glass, silicon, copper plates and a machine casing.$(br2)Every higher tier consumes the previous panel as its core ingredient, forming a $(thing)T1 -> T10$() upgrade chain.

$(bold)Day / Night / Buffer FE:$()$(br)$(li)T1 Silicon: 8 / 2 / 8k$(li)T2 Carbon: 64 / 16 / 64k$(li)T3 Composite: 512 / 128 / 512k$(li)T4 Kronit: 4,096 / 1,024 / 4.096M$(li)T5 Nanocrystalline: 32,768 / 8,192 / 32.768M$(li)T6 Quantum: 128,000 / 32,000 / 128M$(li)T7 Singularity: 512,000 / 128,000 / 512M$(li)T8 Antimatter: 2,500,000 / 625,000 / 250M$(li)T9 Stellar: 10,000,000 / 2,500,000 / 1B$(li)T10 Dyson: 50,000,000 / 12,500,000 / 2B$(br2)$(o)From T6 the buffer no longer scales with output (T8 holds less than T7) — always drain panels into receivers.$()
industrial_core.guide.entry.panel_combin: The $(thing)Panel Combiner$() is one block standing in for an entire solar farm. It holds up to $(thing)18 slots$() of solar panel $(o)items$() (64 each, so up to $(thing)1,152 panels$()) and emits their summed output from a single $(thing)1,000,000,000 FE$() (1B) buffer.$(br2)It is receive-only and pushes from one block.
Crafted from $(thing)T3 panels$(), $(thing)electrum plates$() and a $(thing)machine casing$().

$(bold)Mechanics & gotchas:$()$(br)$(li)Needs its own $(o)open sky$() — one check governs all inserted panels.$(li)Day/night flips the whole combiner's output at once.$(li)Inserted panels are real items and $(o)drop on break$().$(li)A stack of $(thing)T10$() panels (50M/t x 64 x 18) far exceeds the 1B buffer, so keep it drained on a fat cable.
industrial_core.guide.entry.cables.0: $(thing)Cables$() are pure throughput conduits — they carry energy but store $(o)none$() of it. There are seven tiers, from $(thing)4,096 FE/t$() (Copper) up to effectively unlimited (Netherite Fiber).$(br2)Match the cable to your generator or panel output, or it becomes the bottleneck.
industrial_core.guide.entry.cables.1: Cable recipe shape: 3 bare wires on top over the tier material plus rubber insulation, yielding $(thing)3 cables$().$(br2)Shown here for the $(thing)Copper Cable$(): 3 bare copper wire + 3 rubber.
industrial_core.guide.entry.cables.2: $(bold)Throughput by tier:$()$(br)$(li)Copper: 4,096 FE/t$(li)Reinforced Ferro: 65,536$(li)High-Precision Gold Conductor: 262,144$(li)Crystalline Energy Channel: 1,048,576$(li)Emerald Flux: 4,194,304$(li)Heavy Energy Magistral: 16,777,216$(li)Netherite Fiber: 2,147,483,647 (apex T7)$(br2)Throughput jumps roughly 4-16x per tier. A 50M/t $(thing)T10$() panel demands $(thing)Netherite Fiber$() so it never bottlenecks.
industrial_core.guide.entry.batteries.0: Portable cells store FE in their NBT and charge or discharge at the $(o)same$() rate. Four tiers form an upgrade chain, each consuming the previous one.$(br2)The tooltip shows the current $(thing)Energy X/Y FE$().
industrial_core.guide.entry.batteries.1: The base $(thing)Battery$(): copper and tin plates, redstone and rubber.$(br2)Holds $(thing)10,000 FE$() and transfers at $(thing)1,000 FE/t$().
industrial_core.guide.entry.batteries.2: $(bold)The full ladder (capacity / rate):$()$(br)$(li)$(thing)Battery$(): 10,000 / 1,000$(li)$(thing)Lithium Battery$(): 50,000 / 5,000$(li)$(thing)Energy Crystal$(): 100,000 / 10,000$(li)$(thing)Lapotron Crystal$(): 500,000 / 50,000 (top portable)$(br2)Each tier consumes the prior one. The Energy Crystal and Lapotron also feed the Charging Packs.
industrial_core.guide.entry.battery_char: The $(thing)Battery Charger$() is a sink-only charging block. It has a $(thing)1,000,000 FE$() buffer, takes in up to $(thing)50,000 FE/t$(), and pushes that into the item in its single slot.$(br2)It $(o)cannot$() feed energy back to the grid.
Crafted from $(thing)gold plates$(), $(thing)copper cables$(), two $(thing)batteries$() and a $(thing)machine casing$().

Feed it from a generator over cable and drop in a battery, $(thing)Energy Drill$() or $(thing)Electric Wrench$(). It charges at up to $(thing)50,000 FE/t$() — full-rate for a Lapotron — but each item still respects its $(o)own$() intake limit, so a plain Battery caps at its 1,000 FE/t.
industrial_core.guide.entry.charging_pac: $(thing)Charging Packs$() are wireless cells that passively refill your gear while sitting in your inventory.$(br2)The T1 $(thing)Charging Amulet$() holds $(thing)1,000,000 FE$() and transfers at $(thing)5,000 FE/t$().

$(bold)Charge modes$() cycle with $(thing)Shift + RMB$():$(br)$(li)$(thing)Hand$(): the held item only$(li)$(thing)Hotbar$(): slots 0-8$(li)$(thing)All$(): hotbar + offhand$(br2)Each tick it spreads up to its transfer rate across eligible items, skipping itself. An in-world $(thing)HUD overlay$() shows its bar.

The endgame $(thing)Charging Pack T3$() (consumes T2): kronium alloy, exotic matter and netherite fiber cable.$(br2)It holds $(thing)100,000,000 FE$() and spreads $(thing)100,000 FE/t$() across all your gear.
industrial_core.guide.entry.core_machine: The $(thing)Crusher$(), $(thing)Compressor$(), $(thing)Metal Former$() and $(thing)Extractor$() share one chassis: a $(o)64,000 FE$() buffer, $(o)1,024 FE/t$() intake (receive-only), with each recipe's $(o)FE/t$() and time set by its JSON. A built-in $(bold)0.7x$() time multiplier makes them ~30% faster than nominal.$(br2)Layout: slot 0 input, slot 1 output, slots 2-5 upgrades (accelerators, parallel modules, push/pull). Pipes only insert slot 0 and extract slot 1.$(br2)The $(thing)Crusher$() ($(o)crushing$()) doubles ore into $(bold)2 dust$(); gems crush to $(bold)1$().

The $(thing)Metal Former$() has $(bold)two modes you must toggle$(). $(o)Rolling$() (mode 0) makes $(bold)2 plates$() per ingot (iron ingot -> 2 iron plate, 200t / 50 FE). $(o)Extruding$() (mode 1) makes wires and fibers (crystal ingot -> 2 crystal fiber, 200t / 80 FE).$(br2)A rolling recipe will $(bold)not$() run while the machine is in extruding mode, and vice versa - set the mode to match the recipe.

The $(thing)Compressor$() ($(o)compressing$()) densifies items: gem plates from dusts, $(thing)composite ingot$() -> $(thing)composite$() (200t / 50 FE), and 9->1 block packing.$(br2)Like all core machines it runs at $(o)64,000 FE$() buffer and $(o)1,024 FE/t$() with the built-in $(bold)0.7x$() speedup.

The $(thing)Extractor$() ($(o)extracting$()) is the efficient rubber route: $(bold)1 tree resin -> 3 rubber$() (150t / 30 FE). It also recycles wool back into white wool.$(br2)Same chassis as the other core machines.
industrial_core.guide.entry.electric_fur: The $(thing)Electric Furnace$() runs any $(bold)vanilla furnace recipe$() ($(o)minecraft:smelting$()) - not a custom type.$(br2)It has its own energy profile, not the core chassis: a tiny $(o)10,000 FE$() buffer and a slow $(o)100 FE/t$() intake mark it as an early-game machine. Feed it a steady trickle.
Crafted from $(thing)iron plates$(), $(thing)bare copper wire$(), a vanilla $(thing)furnace$() and a $(thing)machine casing$().

Numbers and quirks: processing $(o)20 FE/t$(), base craft $(o)100 ticks$() (5s), and $(bold)no$() built-in 0.7x speedup that the core machines enjoy.$(br2)It reads $(thing)Accelerators$() and $(thing)Parallel$()/$(thing)Advanced Parallel$() modules in slots 2-5, but $(bold)ignores$() push/pull upgrades. Pipes insert slot 0 / extract slot 1.
industrial_core.guide.entry.ore_washer.0: The $(thing)Ore Washer$() trades water and FE for higher yields: ore -> $(bold)3 dust$() (raw variants -> $(bold)2$()) plus a chance byproduct dust.$(br2)Buffer $(o)64,000 FE$(), intake $(o)1,024 FE/t$(), a $(o)128,000 mB$() $(bold)water-only$() tank. 8 slots: 0 input, 1-3 outputs, 4-7 upgrades. Defaults to 200t @ $(o)40 FE/t$().
industrial_core.guide.entry.ore_washer.1: Crafted from $(thing)iron plates$(), $(thing)glass$(), a $(thing)bucket$(), a $(thing)machine casing$() and an $(thing)electric motor$().
industrial_core.guide.entry.ore_washer.2: The byproduct gate: iron -> $(thing)nickel dust$() @30%, gold/lead -> $(thing)silver dust$() @20%/30%, copper -> $(thing)lithium dust$() @25%.$(br2)$(bold)Silver$(), $(bold)Nickel$() and $(bold)Lithium$() dust come ONLY from washing in early game and unlock the alloy lines. The byproduct rolls independently, and the main dust can still fail when its result chance is below 1.0.
industrial_core.guide.entry.heat_machine: The $(thing)Thermogenerator$() is the heat engine: it $(bold)consumes FE$() and pushes $(bold)heat$() into the machine it FACES (a Blast Furnace or Liquefier). It does $(bold)not$() output FE. Buffer $(o)20,000 FE$(), intake $(o)20,000 FE/t$().$(br2)The slotted component is $(bold)not consumed$() - it only sets the heat tier: none $(o)+3 heat$() @200 FE/t; $(thing)electric motor$() $(o)+5$() @400; $(thing)heat conductor$() $(o)+9$() @800; $(thing)advanced heat exchanger$() $(o)+17$() @2000. Display caps at 3000 (+50/t working, -20/t idle).

The $(thing)Industrial Blast Furnace$() alloys an ingredient with a fuel metal. The two are interchangeable (slots 0-1, output slot 2). It stores $(bold)no FE$() and runs purely on piped heat.$(br2)It won't start until heat $(o)>=$() the recipe's $(bold)requiredHeat$(). Heat ceiling $(o)4000$(), -1/t idle. Slots 3-6 take at most ONE $(thing)Blast Upgrade$() (mutually exclusive) plus item push/pull.

Crafted from $(thing)copper plates$(), $(thing)heat conductors$() and a $(thing)machine casing$().$(br2)The Thermogenerator must physically $(bold)FACE$() the Blast Furnace or Liquefier to deliver heat into it.

The $(thing)Liquefier$() melts 1-2 items into an output fluid, also heat-driven by a facing $(thing)Thermogenerator$(). Buffer $(o)50,000 FE$(), intake $(o)2,000 FE/t$(), output tank $(o)8,000 mB$(), heat ceiling $(o)10,000$() (-1/t idle).$(br2)6 slots: 0-1 inputs, 2-5 upgrades. Honors $(bold)Fluid-Push + Item-Pull$() only. Used for molten amethyst and coolant feedstocks.
industrial_core.guide.entry.fluid_machin: The $(thing)Fluid Enricher$() combines an input fluid with 1-2 reagents into an enriched output fluid. Buffer $(o)50,000 FE$(), intake $(o)1,000 FE/t$(), two $(o)8,000 mB$() tanks, defaults 100t @ $(o)50 FE/t$(). Its capsule slots fill/drain only via the $(thing)Capsule$().$(br2)It is the only source of $(thing)Drill Fluid$() (8 mineral dust + 2 silicon dust + 4000mB water -> 4000mB, 200t/60FE) for the Miner, and $(thing)Coolant$() (8 lapis dust + 1000mB water -> 1000mB, 100t/50FE) for the Reactor.

The 'Lava Generator' is a misnomer: it $(bold)CONSUMES$() $(o)500 FE/t$() to synthesize lava ($(o)2,000 mB$() per 160t cycle) into a $(o)128,000 mB$() tank. Buffer $(o)256,000 FE$(), receive-only. $(bold)Zero power output.$()$(br2)2 upgrade slots (Accelerators + Fluid-Push only). Pair it with a Fluid-Push upgrade or pipes.

The $(thing)Water Generator$() consumes $(o)200 FE/t$() to make water ($(o)8,000 mB$() per 80t cycle) into a $(o)128,000 mB$() tank. Buffer $(o)256,000 FE$().$(br2)Cheaper and faster than the lava generator; it also produces $(bold)no FE$().

The $(thing)Stone Generator$() makes cobble, gravel, sand or obsidian from FE. Buffer $(o)100,000 FE$(), $(o)200 FE/t$() in all modes, two $(o)64,000 mB$() tanks.$(br2)Modes: $(o)Cobblestone$() 40t, $(o)Gravel$() 60t, $(o)Sand$() 40t/100mB water, $(o)Obsidian$() 160t/1000mB water+1000mB lava. It $(bold)STARTS OFF$() - press the run button and pick a mode. Directional Item-Push + Fluid-Pull only.
The $(thing)Phase Splitter$() turns one input into up to $(bold)three different outputs$() at once. Buffer $(o)200,000 FE$(), intake $(o)4,000 FE/t$(). 8 slots: 0 input, 1-3 outputs, 4-7 upgrades. Defaults 400t @ $(o)100 FE/t$().$(br2)Overflow logic: output 1 may spill into slots 2/3 $(o)only$() if those aren't claimed by output 2/3. With parallelism it scales the batch $(bold)down$() rather than stalling. Item-Push/Pull only.

The $(thing)Rod Fabricator$() combines two ingredients - notably the nuclear $(thing)fuel rods$() (an empty rod + fissile material). Buffer $(o)100,000 FE$(), intake $(o)2,000 FE/t$(). 7 slots: 0-1 inputs, 2 output, 3-6 upgrades. Defaults 200t @ $(o)500 FE/t$().

The $(thing)Matter Fabricator$() is a pure T6 $(bold)energy sink$() with a $(o)100,000,000 FE$() buffer and $(o)10,000,000 FE/t$() intake. It spends $(bold)50,000,000 FE$() to print one $(thing)Exotic Matter$().$(br2)This is the single most expensive operation in the mod and the late-game currency. Slot 0 is the exotic matter output; slot 1 takes only an Item Pusher. It cannot extract energy.

Why Exotic Matter matters: at $(o)50,000,000 FE$() each it feeds $(thing)Singularity Cores$(), the $(thing)Stellar Matrix$(), cheap $(thing)Kronit$() (4 per 5M via the Restructor), $(thing)netherite$() and $(thing)nether stars$().$(br2)Feed the fabricator from a serious grid. Drop an $(thing)Item Pusher$() in slot 1 to auto-eject the matter.
industrial_core.guide.entry.restructor.0: The $(thing)Restructor$() is an endgame transmuter ($(o)molecular_transforming$()) that turns one material into a rarer one: $(thing)coal block$() -> $(thing)diamond$(), $(thing)iron block$() -> $(thing)kronit$(), $(thing)exotic matter$() -> $(thing)nether star$(). It $(bold)requires a catalyst$() in slot 2 and enormous FE.$(br2)5 slots: 0 input, 1 output, 2 catalyst, 3-4 item push/pull only. There are 17 transmute recipes.
industrial_core.guide.entry.restructor.1: How it runs: it $(bold)charges up$() to the recipe's full FE cost ($(o)750k$() to $(o)25M$()), transmutes instantly, then resets to 0 - there is no normal progress bar, just $(o)Charge: X/Y FE$(). It only sips power while an input item is present. $(bold)MAX_RECEIVE 10,000,000$(), receive-only.$(br2)Upgrade slots 3-4 take only item pushers/pullers - $(bold)no$() accelerators or parallel modules.
industrial_core.guide.entry.restructor.2: The $(thing)Basic Catalyst$() (Tier I). Catalysts gate which recipes run and are $(bold)not consumed$() (stack 16).$(br2)Tier maps to recipe value: $(o)Basic$() (cheap - coal block -> diamond, 750k) -> $(o)Activated$() (~3M) -> $(o)Resonant$() (iron -> kronit, 25M) -> $(o)Universal$() (matter -> nether star).
industrial_core.guide.entry.restructor.3: The $(thing)Universal Catalyst$() (Tier IV) upgrades a $(thing)Resonant Catalyst$() with $(thing)exotic matter$().$(br2)It is required for $(thing)exotic matter$() -> $(thing)nether star$() (4 matter, 500t/15M) and other endgame transmutes.
industrial_core.guide.entry.cultivator.0: The $(thing)Cultivator$() does $(bold)nothing$() without a special upgrade in slot 2. With one installed it either $(o)duplicates plants$() (free - input kept) or $(o)grows geodes/minerals$().$(br2)Buffer $(o)50,000 FE$(), intake $(o)2,000 FE/t$(), a $(o)128,000 mB$() water-only tank using $(o)1,000 mB$() per recipe. Defaults 100t @ $(o)200 FE/t$(). 7 slots: 0 input, 1 output, 2 special upgrade, 3-6 default upgrades.
industrial_core.guide.entry.cultivator.1: Crafted from $(thing)glass$(), $(thing)bone meal$(), $(thing)iron plates$(), a $(thing)machine casing$() and a $(thing)microcontroller$().$(br2)Slots 3-6 accept only Accelerators, Parallel/Advanced-Parallel, Fluid-Pull and Item-Push.
industrial_core.guide.entry.cultivator.2: The $(thing)Biostimulator$() sets $(bold)PLANT mode$(): it duplicates living crops by tag (the $(o)industrial_core:growable_plants$() tag duplicates 1:1 without an explicit recipe), keeping the input - free duplication that doesn't limit parallelism.$(br2)Inert in any other machine.
industrial_core.guide.entry.cultivator.3: The $(thing)Crystalline Resonator$() sets $(bold)MINERAL mode$(): it grows geodes via growing recipes (e.g. budding amethyst -> shards).$(br2)It is $(bold)mutually exclusive$() with the Biostimulator (a single special slot) and inert outside the Cultivator.
industrial_core.guide.entry.logistics.0: $(thing)Item Connectors$() move items between machine faces with no pipes at all. Mount one on a face and set it to $(o)PUSH$() to actively extract from the inventory it faces, or $(o)PULL$() to passively receive.$(br2)A PUSH connector distributes its haul $(o)round-robin$() across every linked PULL connector. Base rate: every $(thing)20t$() it moves up to $(thing)8 items$() per cycle.$(br2)Each connector has a $(thing)15-slot$() ghost filter (whitelist by default; NBT and damage matching off) and $(thing)1$() upgrade slot. $(o)Item and fluid connectors only link to their own kind$() - bridge mixed types through a $(thing)Logistics Node$().
industrial_core.guide.entry.logistics.1: The $(thing)Logistics Linker$() is the wand that wires connectors together. $(o)RMB$() the first block to set it as the source, then $(o)RMB$() the second to link them (range $(thing)<=64 blocks$(), compatible types only). The link is $(o)bidirectional$().$(br2)$(o)RMB$() the same block again to reset a pending link. $(o)Shift+RMB$() a block with a pending source cancels it; $(o)Shift+RMB$() a block with no pending source clears $(bold)ALL$() of its links.
industrial_core.guide.entry.logistics.2: The $(thing)Logistics Node$() is a hub: it joins many connectors into a single network and is the $(o)only$() way to bridge mixed connector types (item to fluid).$(br2)Its link list doubles as $(o)distribution priority$() - drag entries to reorder them, and click an entry to highlight that block in the world.
industrial_core.guide.entry.logistics.3: $(thing)Connector Upgrades$() (one per connector) speed up the cycle and raise the batch size:$(br)$(li)$(thing)T1$(): every $(thing)10t$(), $(thing)16$() items / $(thing)1000 mB$()$(li)$(thing)T2$(): every $(thing)5t$(), $(thing)32$() items / $(thing)2000 mB$()$(li)$(thing)T3$(): every $(thing)5t$(), $(thing)64$() items / $(thing)10000 mB$()$(br2)Note: $(thing)T3$() over $(thing)T2$() is $(o)batch-only$() - both run at the same 5-tick interval.
industrial_core.guide.entry.machine_upgr: $(thing)Accelerators$() trade energy for speed, and they $(o)stack multiplicatively$():$(br)$(li)$(thing)Accelerator$(): -30% time / +30% FE$(li)$(thing)Accelerator Mk1$(): -50% time / +50% FE$(li)$(thing)Accelerator Mk2$(): -75% time / +100% FE$(br2)Drop them into any machine's upgrade slots (most base machines expose $(thing)4$()). The base Accelerator needs three $(o)coolant-FILLED$() $(thing)Capsules$(), so fill your capsules first.

$(thing)Directional$() pushers and pullers let a machine feed and eject itself without connectors. There are four variants: $(thing)Item Pusher$(), $(thing)Item Puller$(), $(thing)Fluid Pusher$() and $(thing)Fluid Puller$().$(br2)$(o)Right-click a block face$() to store that direction in the upgrade. Until you do, the upgrade $(o)does nothing$().

The $(thing)Parallel Module$() batches up to $(thing)x8$() operations per craft and works in $(o)every$() base machine.$(br2)$(4)Critical gotcha:$() the $(thing)Advanced Parallel Module$() ($(thing)x32$()) and $(thing)Quantum Parallel Module$() ($(thing)x64$()) are $(o)inert$() in ordinary machines. Only specially-aware machines - like the $(thing)Capsule Filler$() - honor them.
industrial_core.guide.entry.capsule_syst: The $(thing)Universal Capsule$() is a portable fluid carrier holding $(thing)1000 mB$() that stacks to $(thing)64$(). $(o)Right-click a fluid source block$() to scoop it; the capsule's name then shows the fluid it holds.$(br2)$(4)Anti-dupe rule:$() a $(o)full$() capsule cannot be emptied by hand. Route it through machines or the $(thing)Capsule Filler$() to fill and drain it.
Crafting capsules: $(thing)3 Tin Plates$() yield $(thing)4 Universal Capsules$().

The $(thing)Capsule Filler$() fills empty containers - Voltex capsules, vanilla buckets, or any mod's fluid-handler item - from its internal $(thing)128,000 mB$() tank. Each fill uses $(thing)1000 mB$() over $(thing)40t$() base.$(br2)It is one of the few machines that honor the $(thing)Advanced$() ($(thing)x32$()) and $(thing)Quantum$() ($(thing)x64$()) parallel modules, and it reads Accelerators and all four directional pushers/pullers.$(br2)The tank is $(o)fill-only$() via pipes, and the machine $(o)waits for a full 1000 mB portion$() before it fills a container.
industrial_core.guide.entry.nexite_armor: $(thing)Nexite$() is the utility and defence set. Each piece holds $(bold)200,000 FE$() and never loses durability $(o)at all$() - instead it spends energy to soak incoming hits, draining $(bold)100 FE$() per point of damage absorbed.$(br2)At $(bold)0 FE$() the armour goes completely inert, so keep it charged.

Damage absorption scales with the pieces worn: $(li)1 piece - $(bold)50%$()$(li)2 pieces - $(bold)60%$()$(li)3 pieces - $(bold)80%$()$(li)4 pieces - $(bold)90%$()$(br)The $(thing)boots$() negate all fall damage past the first 4 blocks. A full charged set grants permanent $(thing)Haste I$(), refreshed every second.
$(thing)Некситовый нагрудник$() is built from $(thing)kronit$() around an $(thing)energy crystal$() core. All four pieces follow the same kronit + energy-crystal pattern.
industrial_core.guide.entry.plasma_armor: $(thing)Plasma$() is the offence and mobility set. Each piece holds $(bold)1,000,000 FE$() and never breaks.$(br2)Wearing the $(bold)full 4-piece set$() grants $(thing)creative-style flight$() - remove any piece and flight switches off immediately.

Absorption scales with $(o)charged$() pieces: 25 / 50 / 75 / $(bold)100%$() for 1-4. A full charged set grants $(thing)Fire Resistance$().$(br2)$(bold)Plasma Burst$(): every point of absorbed damage adds 1 to the counter. When all pieces hit 500 (2,000 total absorbed), it detonates for $(bold)100 damage$() to every hostile within $(bold)8 blocks$(), then resets. Keep all four topped up.
$(thing)Плазменный нагрудник$() is built from $(thing)kronit$() and $(thing)kronium alloy$(), with a $(thing)lapotron crystal$() and a $(thing)data transfer matrix$() core.
industrial_core.guide.entry.storage_bloc: $(thing)Crates$() are chest-style item storage in three tiers: $(bold)54 / 72 / 90$() slots. Each exposes an item handler, so connectors and pipes can fill and empty them automatically.$(br2)Tiered crafting consumes the previous crate. Contents drop when the block is broken.
The $(thing)Промышленный ящик$() ($(bold)90 slots$()) surrounds a $(thing)Укреплённый ящик$() core with $(thing)aluminium plates$(), a $(thing)logic board$() and $(thing)hardened steel$().

$(thing)Barrels$() each store a $(o)single$() fluid type across three tiers: $(bold)32k / 128k / 512k mB$(). They keep their fluid when broken and expose a fluid handler for connectors.$(br2)Fill or drain with a $(thing)bucket$() or $(thing)capsule$(); an empty-hand right-click just shows a read-out. The T1 $(thing)Бочка для жидкости$() is 8 $(thing)tin plates$().
industrial_core.guide.entry.miner.0: The $(thing)Miner$() is a $(bold)3x3x3$() fabricator that synthesizes random ore blocks rather than digging the world.$(br2)At its heart sits the $(thing)Miner Core$(); the $(o)Lens$() in slot 0 decides which dimension's ore pool it draws from. Each ore costs $(bold)1000 FE/t$() and $(bold)1000 mB$() of $(thing)Drill Fluid$(), taking 200t at base speed.
industrial_core.guide.entry.miner.1: Build a solid $(bold)3x3x3$() cube of $(bold)27$() blocks: place the $(thing)Miner Core$() dead center and surround it with $(bold)26$() $(thing)Miner Casing$() on every face, edge and corner.$(br2)The structure $(o)auto-forms$() once complete (it re-checks every 40t) and the casings turn $(o)invisible$(). Breaking any single casing instantly unforms it. Right-click the core or any casing to open the GUI.
industrial_core.guide.entry.miner.2: The $(thing)Miner Casing$() forms the 26-block shell.$(br2)Each craft yields $(bold)2$(), so $(bold)13$() crafts give you the full $(bold)26$() you need for one Miner.
industrial_core.guide.entry.miner.3: $(thing)Lenses$() (slot 0) select the ore pool: $(thing)Lens: Overworld$(), $(thing)Lens: Nether$() and $(thing)Lens: The End$(). The $(thing)Universal Lens$() is crafted shapeless from all three lenses plus a $(thing)nether star$() and covers everything. The Miner also auto-absorbs other mods' ores via $(o)forge:ores$() tags.$(br2)$(thing)Drill Fluid$() has $(bold)only one$() recipe, in the $(thing)Fluid Enricher$(): $(bold)8$() mineral dust + $(bold)2$() silicon dust + $(bold)4000 mB$() water yields $(bold)4000 mB$(). The core holds 16,000 mB.$(br2)Slots 1-4 take $(thing)Accelerators$() to speed up each ore.
industrial_core.guide.entry.miner.4: $(bold)CRITICAL output rule.$() The Miner only auto-pushes finished ores into storage touching the $(o)outer faces$() of the cube.$(br2)Place a $(thing)chest$() or $(thing)barrel$() flush against the outer skin (or a hopper/pipe pulling from any face). The cube exposes an extract-only item handler on every outer face.$(br2)With $(o)no adjacent storage$() the 8-slot output buffer fills, and the Miner silently $(bold)stops$(). It only ticks while formed, with a Lens, at least 1000 FE, drill fluid present, and a non-full output.
industrial_core.guide.entry.reactor.0: The $(thing)Nuclear Reactor$() is a $(bold)3x3x3$() multiblock with a $(bold)5x5$() (25-slot) component grid. Fuel rods generate $(bold)FE$() and $(bold)heat$(), scaled by a neutron multiplier; cooling components manage that heat.$(br2)It buffers $(bold)10,000,000 FE$() ($(o)extract-only$() — it cannot receive). If hull heat reaches $(bold)500,000$() the reactor $(bold)explodes$() with radius $(bold)20$(), igniting fire like TNT.
industrial_core.guide.entry.reactor.1: Build a $(bold)3x3x3$() cube: the $(thing)Nuclear Reactor$() controller dead center, surrounded by $(bold)26$() $(thing)Reactor Chamber$() blocks.$(br2)Power is drawn from $(o)any$() outer chamber face — all 54 outer faces distribute energy, so attach your cables anywhere on the skin. The structure auto-forms when complete. Open the GUI from the controller or any chamber.$(br2)The $(thing)Reactor Chamber$() crafts $(bold)2$() at a time, so $(bold)13$() crafts cover all 26.
industrial_core.guide.entry.reactor.2: Fuel rods are made in the $(thing)Rod Fabricator$():$(br)$(li)$(thing)Uranium$() — 10,000 FE/t, 30 heat/t, 960s$(li)$(thing)Plutonium$() — 25,000 FE/t, 50 heat/t, 480s$(li)$(thing)MOX$() — 100,000 FE/t, 100 heat/t, 720s$(br)Durability is the lifetime in seconds; once depleted the rod swaps to its $(o)depleted$() variant, which breaks the scheme and stops the reactor.$(br2)$(bold)Neutron multiplier:$() each adjacent fuel rod $(o)or$() graphite rod adds $(bold)+1$() to both power and heat.
industrial_core.guide.entry.reactor.3: $(bold)Schemes are the run-gate.$() The grid must $(o)exactly$() match an inserted $(thing)Scheme$() item, or no energy flows. The $(bold)11$() schemes span $(bold)20,000 FE/t$() (Uranium I) up to $(bold)2,400,000 FE/t$() (MOX IV).$(br2)Cooling components keep cells under $(bold)50,000$() and the hull under $(bold)500,000$(): heat vents (V/C/I/A), heat exchangers (X/Y), graphite rods (G) and the single-use condensator (K).$(br2)The $(thing)Reactor Blueprint$() saves a custom layout to NBT but is $(bold)not$() a scheme — it won't gate the reactor on.
industrial_core.guide.entry.reactor.4: $(bold)Cooling & safety.$() Natural hull cooling is only $(bold)-5/t$() — far too slow on its own.$(br2)Active cooling kicks in above $(bold)10,000$() hull heat: every 20t it drains $(bold)100 mB$() of cold $(thing)Coolant$() (made in the $(thing)Fluid Enricher$() from lapis dust), removes $(bold)5000$() hull heat, and emits $(bold)100 mB$() of $(thing)Hot Coolant$() (recyclable).$(br2)A $(thing)condensator$() is $(o)single-use$(): deleted once it has absorbed 500,000, which breaks the scheme. Keep cold coolant piped in — hull at $(bold)500,000$() means $(bold)detonation$().
tooltip.industrial_core.electric_wrench: Shift+RMB on machine — remove without drops
msg.industrial_core.reactor_not_formed: Reactor not assembled — requires 26 Reactor Chamber blocks (3×3×3)
tooltip.industrial_core.resin_collector: Place next to the bottom log of a resin tree
tooltip.industrial_core.barrel_hint: Right-click with a bucket or capsule to fill/drain
tooltip.industrial_core.blast_upgrade_on: Works only in the Blast Furnace
tooltip.industrial_core.direction_not_se: Not configured — RMB on block face
tooltip.industrial_core.graphite_rod: Neutron reflector (+1 to adjacent rod multipliers)
tooltip.industrial_core.advanced_heat_ex: Fast heat exchange with adjacent cells
tooltip.industrial_core.reactor_exchange: Up to %s heat/t to adjacent cells
tooltip.industrial_core.blueprint_empty: Empty — use GUI button to save layout
tooltip.industrial_core.universal_cataly: Tier IV Restructor catalyst — prints the rarest from matter
tooltip.industrial_core.nuclear_reactor: Structure: 3×3×3 with the Reactor in the center.
tooltip.industrial_core.miner_core: Structure: 3×3×3 with the Core in the center.
msg.industrial_core.miner_not_formed: Structure incomplete. Surround the core with Miner Casing (3x3x3).
tooltip.industrial_core.plasma_info: Charges from damage • Full charge: Plasma Burst • Full set: Fire Resistance
jei.industrial_core.capsule_filler.any: Any container with a tank (buckets/cells of other mods)
msg.industrial_core.linker.source_select: Source selected. RMB target to connect
